articles.title: GETTY GETS A PUNCHY MODERN REBRAND ANCHORED BY GEOMETRIC "G" MOTIF
articles.summary: The Getty has unveiled a modern rebrand developed with Fred & Farid New York that features a geometric "G" icon anchored by the tagline "ALL FOR ART." The flexible "G" forms a square block referencing the Getty Center's travertine architecture, with four mosaic-inspired pieces representing Getty's core programs. The new visual identity emphasizes Getty's commitment to expanding global access to art and cultural heritage through a unified, adaptable design system.

articles.title: A NEW PLATFORM "BY CREATORS, FOR CREATORS" WILL ROOT OUT AI DEEPFAKES
articles.summary: FanLock is a new platform co-founded by sketch comedian Zander Small to help creators identify and remove AI deepfakes across over four million websites. The service costs $49+ per month and specifically targets platforms like Telegram where deepfakes are commonly shared, addressing a problem that existing DMCA services haven't fully solved. With AI fraud losses predicted to reach $40 billion by 2027, the platform aims to provide creators with better tools to protect their likeness from unauthorized AI-generated content.

articles.title: APPLE OFFICIALLY ANNOUNCES IPHONE 17E WITH MAGSAFE, 256GB STORAGE, MORE
articles.summary: Apple announced the iPhone 17e, a more affordable model in the iPhone 17 lineup featuring the new A19 chip, Apple's C1X modem (up to 2× faster than before), MagSafe charging, a 48MP camera with 2x optical-quality zoom, and a new 256GB base storage. It starts at $599 (256GB), with a 512GB option at $799, comes in black, white, and soft pink, opens for pre-order March 4, and launches March 11.

articles.title: BREAKING THE ECHO CHAMBER IN YOUR INTERFACE
articles.summary: Chatbots trained through reinforcement learning from human feedback tend to validate users' ideas regardless of merit, creating conversational echo chambers where agreement replaces genuine analysis. Designing interfaces with productive friction can counteract this sycophancy. The level of friction should scale with stakes: casual tasks warrant smooth interactions, while high-stakes decisions call for deliberate cognitive speed bumps.

articles.title: THE WISDOM CURVE
articles.summary: A 70-year-old consultant thriving in a technical environment illustrates how long careers can evolve from craft to leadership to systems thinking, combining deep institutional knowledge with curiosity for new fields like AI. Using Maslow's self-transcendence, the Toolset–Skillset–Mindset framework, and the shift from fluid to crystallized intelligence, the piece argues that sustainable careers are built on wisdom, mindset, and contribution—not speed or tools alone—and encourages professionals to grow from innovators into mentors.

articles.title: DESIGN STUDIOS SHOULD BE PUTTING ON THEIR OWN EXHIBITIONS. HERE'S WHY
articles.summary: Design studios today are defined not just by what they produce, but by the ideas they question and the futures they help shape. Exhibitions offer a powerful alternative to algorithm-driven portfolios by creating immersive, intentional spaces for dialogue and cultural contribution. By showcasing process, point of view, and speculative thinking, exhibitions help studios own their narrative, build deeper relationships, and position themselves not just by what they've done, but by what they are becoming.

articles.title: AI UGC VIDEO GENERATOR
articles.summary: Reloop is an AI video generator that creates UGC ads in under 30 seconds without requiring prompts or technical skills. The platform includes a Composer Agent that plans and builds customized video content featuring AI avatars, voice cloning, and auto captions.

articles.title: DETECT OUTRAGE BAIT PATTERNS
articles.summary: RageCheck is a tool designed to identify outrage bait patterns in content. It helps users recognize when material is specifically crafted to provoke anger or strong emotional reactions.

articles.title: WARGAMES TERMINAL FONTS
articles.summary: WarGames Terminal Fonts is a manual transcription of characters from terminal displays shown in the 1983 movie WarGames. The font pack includes 15 files in various formats with three variants: Normal, Double (vertically stretched), and Raster (simulating scan lines).

articles.title: HOW TO PROTECT YOUR CREATIVE PROCESS WHEN REAL LIFE GETS DISTRACTING
articles.summary: When life gets distracting, protect your creative process by taking breaks when emotionally overwhelmed, working in short, focused bursts, and collaborating with others for fresh perspectives. Say no to draining projects and use templates when your brain feels fried to maintain productivity without burnout. Handle non-creative tasks like legal matters early so they don't interfere with your creative flow later.

articles.title: HOW TO WRITE A GOOD SPEC FOR AI AGENTS
articles.summary: Writing effective AI agent specs requires clear, structured documents covering six core areas — commands, testing, project structure, code style, git workflow, and boundaries — while keeping them focused enough to avoid overwhelming the model. Large specs should be broken into modular, task-specific prompts rather than delivered all at once. The process works best as a continuous loop: plan first in read-only mode, execute incrementally, enforce three-tier constraints, and refine the spec alongside the code as the project evolves.

articles.title: CANVA ISN'T A JOKE ANYMORE
articles.summary: Once dismissed as a casual design tool, Canva is rapidly repositioning itself as a professional creative suite through acquisitions like Affinity and motion design software Cavalry, signaling ambitions to compete more directly with Adobe and potentially become the first credible all-in-one alternative to Creative Cloud.

articles.title: POKÉMON DAY 2026: WHAT ARTISTS CAN LEARN FROM 30 YEARS OF POKÉMON CHARACTER DESIGN
articles.summary: Game Freak and Nintendo's 30-year Pokémon character design demonstrates that technical constraints can enhance creativity rather than limit it.

articles.title: WHAT DOES A THOUGHTFUL AI POLICY LOOK LIKE IN DESIGN EDUCATION?
articles.summary: Design students are already using generative AI, so schools must develop policies that thoughtfully integrate it while preserving core educational goals such as authorship, critical thinking, and individual voice.

articles.title: GODZILLA INSPIRED THIS ARTIST'S DEPICTIONS OF MASSIVE MACHINERY IN NATURAL ENVIRONMENTS
articles.summary: YuCong Tang is a Chinese concept designer who uses Photoshop and Procreate with direct brushstrokes to create painterly worlds blending massive machinery and natural environments often inspired by fantasy, sci-fi, horror films, polar exploration, and industrial airships.
